details.summary: Gate-5 write-up complete for graffiti-143 (WoW Conjecture 143: variance of positive eigenvalues <= size/average distance — REFUTED). WRITEUP.md follows FRAMEWORK.md §9: verbatim frozen statement + decode provenance (wow-july2004.pdf, sha256, cmsy non-strict <=); five counterexample instances inline (dumbbell family, smallest certified n=39 dumbbell(7,12,20) for both average-distance readings, n=37 dumbbell(6,12,19) for distinct-pairs; exact rational margins +0.0804/+0.9582 etc.); verification procedure (dual-route exact checker checker_g143.py — sympy isolation + pure-stdlib Berkowitz/quotient-identity/Yun/Sturm at 2^-70, no floats on accept path; 10/10 mutation kills; orchestrator verify_kills.py §[143] independent rebuild; Codex GPT-5.5 hostile-referee ACCEPT) plus a tested 20-line standalone numpy float sanity snippet (verbatim-extracted from the .md and re-run: reproduces 8/4976/34.3151>34.2347>33.3569); extension findings (n=39/37 supersede claim-record n=40; family-exhaustive float minimality n<=48; heuristic beyond-family probe; robust-reading checks; LHS/RHS->2 asymptotics); what-this-does-not-show (no general minimality below n=37, n=90 instance float-only, reading dependence disclosed); fresh kill-check section dated 2026-06-11 with 9 documented queries (all empty); publication grouping: bundle with graffiti-154 as the two WoW spectral kills (one short note, natural follow-up to Roucairol–Cazenave ECAI 2025), separate from the three solubilizer kills (a1/a13/a16) and the two TxGraffiti-family kills (davila-conj9, pandey-parity). Abstract is 129 words. Checker and mutation suite both re-passed fresh during this Gate-5 pass.

details.summary: Gate-5 write-up complete for graffiti-154. WRITEUP.md follows FRAMEWORK section 9: verbatim frozen statement (WoW July 2004, glyph-decoded) with full convention provenance (deviation = population std dev per FMS 1993 + the only machine formalization; both average-distance conventions covered); counterexamples inline as an integer table — lollipop(48,70) n=118 (DP only), lollipop(50,70) n=120 and lollipop(72,72) n=144 (both conventions) via 8mW^2 > n^5(n-1)^2 / n^7, plus a 7-line stranger-verifiable integer snippet (executed, passes); verification procedure covering checker A (check_graffiti154.py, fresh full run today: ALL CHECKS PASS), Codex's independent checker B (codex_referee_audit.py), the orchestrator's verify_kills.py [154] section (re-run today: all PASS), six mutation tests, and the verbatim-archived Codex KILL CONFIRMED verdict; extension findings (non-uniqueness of minimal instances, N2-implies-DP structural robustness, MAD non-violation upgraded to exact Sturm/Koolen-Moulton proofs, FMS provenance re-fetch, IASE-at-ECAI-2025 venue correction); explicit what-this-does-not-show (kill claimed ONLY under the std-dev reading; MAD reading provably not violated by these instances; minimality only within the lollipop family; nothing about neighboring conjectures). Publication grouping line: bundle with graffiti-143 as the two Graffiti spectral kills (same source, openness baseline, clique+path mechanism — one short paper); distinct sibling bundles are the three solubilizer kills (a1/a13/a16) and the two TxGraffiti-family kills (davila-conj9, pandey-parity). Fresh same-day kill-check: clean (see killCheck field).

details.summary: Gate-5 write-up completed for the verified two-direction refutation of Conjecture 4.1 of arXiv:2601.03293 (Pandey parity conjecture: I(GP(n,k),x) real-rooted iff k even). WRITEUP.md follows the FRAMEWORK.md section 9 claim standard: frozen verbatim statement + provenance (arXiv abs/html, accessed 2026-06-11); counterexamples inline — GP(9,2) (k even, I = 1+18x+126x^2+438x^3+801x^4+747x^5+303x^6+27x^7, exactly 5 real roots by exact Sturm, kills 'if'), GP(7,3) and GP(3,1) (k odd, real-rooted, kill 'only if'), and the explicit isomorphism GP(7,2)≅GP(7,3) (u_j→v_{3j mod 7}, v_j→u_{3j mod 7}) showing the predicate is not isomorphism-invariant; verification procedure (clean-room check_pandey_parity.py with --selftest mutation tests, independent Rust brute force bf_gp.rs, orchestrator verify_kills.py section 5, Codex hostile-referee verdict near-verbatim); extension findings (exhaustive exact scan 3<=n<=14: violations exactly (3,1),(7,3),(9,2),(9,4),(10,2),(11,2),(11,4),(13,4),(14,2),(14,4),(14,6)); calibrated what-this-does-not-show section (insufficient-parameter-coverage framing per verification-report nit, no claim against author's tested window, log-concavity not addressed, kill-check is 'none found' not 'none exists'); ~120-word abstract; and a publication-grouping line recommending bundling with the two Graffiti spectral kills (graffiti-143, graffiti-154), with the two TxGraffiti-family davila-conj9 kills as alternative and solubilizer kills excluded as off-topic. Checker re-run today: --selftest exit 0, full run exit 0, 'ALL CHECKS PASSED'.
